teaching.concept: Italics make a word or short phrase stand out without making it bold.
teaching.howTo: Wrap the words with one asterisk or one underscore on each side.
teaching.example: *Quiet music*

candidates.title: Italic emphasis
candidates.prompt: Write one short phrase in italics using Markdown.
candidates.target: *Blue marble*

matchChecks.feedback: Make at least one phrase italic with Markdown.

editorialChecks.review: Keep italics focused on one short phrase in this exercise.

hints: Italic text has one matching marker on each side.
Type one asterisk, the phrase, then one more asterisk.
Example: `*Quiet music*`

teaching.concept: A quotation card can combine a title, an italic cue, and a message from a named source.
teaching.howTo: Use one H1, one italic paragraph, then a quote with text that starts with `>`.
teaching.example:
# Coach's message

*Remember this before the game.*

> Coach: Bring a water bottle.

candidates.title: Rebuild a reminder card
candidates.prompt: Rebuild the rendered document as Markdown. Your wording may differ, but keep the same Markdown shape.
candidates.target:
# Baking reminder

*Prepare the pan before mixing.*

> Recipe note: Let the bread cool before slicing.

matchChecks.feedback: Rebuild the three-block shape shown in the Goal: title, italic note, then the final block.
Make the middle note italic with Markdown.
Finish with a Markdown quote that contains text.

editorialChecks.review: Keep italics focused on one short phrase in this exercise.

hints: Start with one # title.
Put one complete sentence in italics below the title.
Finish with one quoted message that starts with `>`.

teaching.concept: A small practical note can combine a title, an italic sentence, and a bullet list.
teaching.howTo: Use one H1, one italic paragraph, then an unordered list with at least two items.
teaching.example:
# Art box

*Bring a few tools for drawing.*

- Paper
- Crayons

candidates.title: Rebuild a quick note
candidates.prompt: Rebuild the rendered document as Markdown. Your wording may differ, but keep the same Markdown shape.
candidates.target:
# Cat supplies

*Check these before dinner.*

- Fresh water
- Dry food

matchChecks.feedback: Rebuild the three-block shape shown in the Goal: title, italic note, then the final block.
Make the middle note italic with Markdown.
Finish with an unordered list of at least two items.

editorialChecks.review: Keep italics focused on one short phrase in this exercise.

hints: Start with one # title.
Put one complete sentence in italics below the title.
Finish with at least two bullet items that start with `-`.

teaching.concept: A short process is easier to follow when a title and italic purpose lead into numbered steps.
teaching.howTo: Use one H1, one italic paragraph, then an ordered list with at least three steps.
teaching.example:
# Plant a seed

*Follow these steps in order.*

1. Fill the pot
2. Add the seed
3. Water the soil
